behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is module handoff: commit-diff computation + age-threshold evaluation + PR-state check. This is not a single-function concern (so above Unit), but the behavior text does not require live OS/network/subprocess. REJECTION-GATE: I considered System, but the behavior does not say "live repo" or real API; in-process wiring with realistic fixtures can expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire staleness detector with real internal modules and faked repo/PR providers. Build cases for: (a) commits ahead + older than threshold + no PR => stale, (b) ahead but fresh => not stale, (c) no ahead commits => not stale. Assert final stale decision and intermediate state handoff are consistent.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This checks startup orchestration and message composition across modules (state detector -> bootstrap -> warning renderer), not just one pure function. No explicit real OS/network/subprocess dependency is stated. REJECTION-GATE: I considered System because it is "session-start," but behavior text does not require real process startup mechanics; module-level integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initializer in-process with a stubbed stale branch state and real warning-formatting code. Assert warning is emitted once and includes actionable context (branch name, age, ahead/PR status cues). Verify non-stale state emits no stale warning.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets a local deterministic classifier function with no I/O or cross-module boundary. Escalation would be over-testing the boundary described.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive branch-state inputs into the classifier and assert exact label outputs for all relevant combinations, including edge conditions at threshold boundaries.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live repo and live PR state. Real git/process/network interactions are the failure boundary; mocks can hide integration mismatches and real-state edge behavior. REJECTION-GATE: I considered Integration, but "against live repo and PR state" directly disqualifies demotion.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo (real git history) with real PR-state source (or real API in a test environment). Validate rendered output shows `clean` for synchronized branch and `STALE` wording for branch ahead with no PR.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is state-contract handling between branch analyzer and PR-status interpretation modules. This requires module wiring but not necessarily real network or subprocess. REJECTION-GATE: I considered System, but behavior text does not require live PR API, only correct classification for open/merged states.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate staleness evaluator with PR-state mapper using representative open/merged/no-PR fixtures. Assert open and merged states force non-stale-no-PR outcomes even when branch has old commits ahead; no-PR case remains stale when other conditions match.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: This behavior hinges on real VCS history semantics under rebase/squash (hash rewrites, ancestry/cherry equivalence), which commonly fail only with real git behavior. In-process mocks often miss these graph semantics. REJECTION-GATE: I considered Integration, but the explicit "rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change" points to real git behavior as the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Create real git scenarios: branch commits, then rebase/squash into main so hashes differ from original branch commits. Run staleness detection and assert branch is not classified stale-no-PR after integration into main-equivalent history.
